fields.delivery_address: 3341 Harbor Light Lane, San Pedro, CA 90731
fields.icd_conflict_detail: No conflict detected. ICD-10 code G35 (Multiple sclerosis) is consistently documented across all three documents: the referral form, clinical notes, and prescription.
fields.priority_reason: Patient has a confirmed neurological diagnosis of Multiple sclerosis (G35), which is a progressive neurological condition that automatically warrants HIGH priority per clinical risk classification guidelines.
fields.notes: 1. CLINICAL INCONSISTENCY FLAG: The clinical notes describe 'post-surgical rehabilitation protocol' and a 'workplace injury' with pain levels and restricted range of motion — this is inconsistent with a primary diagnosis of Multiple sclerosis (G35), which is a chronic neurological condition, not a surgical or trauma-related condition. This warrants clinical review to confirm whether the diagnosis, treatment notes, or both are accurate for this claim. 2. WEIGHT CONCERN: Patient weighs 280 lbs at 5'7" (BMI ~43.8). Standard commode chairs (E0163) typically have a weight capacity of 250-300 lbs. A heavy-duty or bariatric commode (E0165 or E0167) may be more appropriate and should be confirmed with the prescribing physician before fulfillment. 3. The referral and prescription were both submitted/signed on 02/06/2026; clinical notes are dated 02/04/2026, which is consistent with normal workflow. 4. Prescription is valid through 05/07/2026 (90 days from 02/06/2026). 5. Duration of need is 3 months per both clinical notes and prescription.

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026
